rationale: The cited paper is used in the theory-development section, not just in a background list. Specifically, it is cited (with related work) to support the claim that consumers evaluate their own effort in relation to firm effort, which the authors use to build hypotheses about responses to firm conservation requests. Although it is grouped with other citations, its functional role is clear and contributes to the conceptual foundation of the model.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Kivetz and Simonson (2002) as part of the dual-entitlement/effort literature to justify that perceived firm effort shapes consumer willingness to exert effort. This premise underpins the citing paper's predictions about reactance, green perceptions, and conservation behavior.
